The '''Model 44 blaster pistol''' was a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] manufactured by [[Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc./Legends|Merr-Sonn Munitions, Inc.]] prior to the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="CCG Premiere" /> This model was similar to the [[Power 5 heavy blaster pistol]].

The Model 44 was a low-powered [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], featuring a shorter [[Barrel/Legends|barrel]] than the Power 5. This weapon was carried by [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officers]] aboard the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|first Death Star]]. The Model 44 was also the pistol carried by [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] during her masquerade as the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh]].

[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Mazer Rackus]] carried a Model 44 blaster pistol.<ref name="GG9">''[[Galaxy Guide 9: Fragments from the Rim]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The original image of this weapon from the ''[[Star Wars Customizable Card Game]]'' appears to be based on an image of the same prop that was the basis for the [[Power 5 heavy blaster pistol]], but incomplete or broken.<ref name="CCG Premiere" /> ''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]'' includes a behind the scenes photo of the incomplete prop captioned "A heavy blaster under construction."<ref name="BtM">''[[Star Wars: Behind the Magic]]''</ref>

Like almost all the other weapon props used in the [[Original trilogy]], the design of the 44 was based on an actual weapon. In this case, the German "Broomhandle" Mauser pistol was used and modified. The Mauser is the same weapon upon which the [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44 heavy blaster pistol]] (popularized by [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]]) was built.

To make the Model 44, various odds and ends were attached to the Mauser, as well as certain pieces (most notably, the Mauser's long barrel) being hacked off.

The [[Power 5 heavy blaster pistol]] was also based on the German Mauser. The most noticeable difference between it and the 44 is the Power 5's flash suppressor and full barrel.
